skill.name: prior-auth-letter-drafter
skill.description: Generate professional prior authorization request letters for insurance companies with proper clinical justification and formatting.
skill.summary: Generates formatted prior authorization letters with ICD-10 codes, clinical justification, and carrier-specific requirements.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 95 potential issues across 8 files, including 51 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' patterns. Manual review confirmed all high-severity findings are false positives: substring matching on 'des' within words like 'codes', 'description', and 'despite' triggered crypto alerts, and markdown backtick formatting triggered shell execution alerts. The skill is a legitimate prior authorization letter generator. Its filesystem operations (reading JSON input, writing text output) are documented, expected, and standard for this tool type. No network access, credential exposure, or command injection risks were found.

low_findings.title: User-controlled file output paths without sanitization
low_findings.description: The --output and --sample-output CLI arguments accept arbitrary file paths. os.makedirs at line 148 creates directories at any user-specified location the process can access. While standard CLI behavior, this allows writing output files outside the intended workspace if a user provides an unexpected path.

content.value_statement: Healthcare providers spend hours writing prior authorization letters for insurance companies. This skill generates professional, clinically-justified letters in minutes using standardized templates and medical terminology.
actual_capabilities: Generate formatted prior authorization letters with provider and patient details
Include ICD-10 and CPT codes with structured clinical justification sections
Support multiple service types including procedures, medications, DME, and imaging
Apply insurance carrier-specific formatting requirements from reference data
Produce editable output files ready for provider review and submission
Create sample input files demonstrating required data fields and format
limitations: Requires complete patient and provider information to generate accurate letters
Cannot verify medical necessity or clinical appropriateness of authorization requests
Output quality depends on the detail and accuracy of the clinical justification input provided
Does not submit letters to insurance companies or track authorization status after generation

use_cases.title: Medical Practice Staff
use_cases.description: Office administrators generate standardized prior authorization letters for common procedures and medications using pre-filled templates and carrier-specific formatting rules.
use_cases.target_user: Medical office administrators and billing staff

use_cases.title: Individual Healthcare Providers
use_cases.description: Physicians and specialists draft detailed clinical justifications for complex authorization requests, saving time while maintaining professional documentation standards.

use_cases.title: Medical Billing Services
use_cases.description: Third-party billing companies produce consistent authorization letters across multiple provider clients using standardized workflows and carrier reference data.
use_cases.target_user: Medical billing and revenue cycle professionals

prompt_templates.title: Basic Procedure Authorization Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a prior authorization letter for a patient needing a standard surgical procedure. Use the sample input format to provide patient details, provider information, ICD-10 codes, and clinical justification.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user learning the basic letter generation workflow

prompt_templates.title: Medication Prior Authorization with Step Therapy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prior authorization request for a specialty medication. Include documentation of failed formulary alternatives, step therapy requirements met, and clinical evidence supporting the requested medication over preferred alternatives.
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting authorization for non-formulary prescription drugs

prompt_templates.title: Carrier-Specific Formatted Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a prior authorization letter following UnitedHealthcare specific requirements. Use the carrier requirements reference to include all mandatory fields such as tax ID, clinical notes reference, and follow their submission formatting guidelines.
prompt_templates.scenario: Tailoring letters to specific insurance company requirements

prompt_templates.title: Complex Multi-Service Authorization Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a comprehensive prior authorization letter covering multiple related services for a patient with complex comorbidities. Include the primary procedure with supporting diagnostic justification, related CPT codes, all applicable ICD-10 codes, failed conservative treatments, and a detailed clinical narrative connecting the patient's history to the requested interventions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex cases requiring authorization for bundled or multi-step services

output_examples.input: Patient with symptomatic cholelithiasis requiring laparoscopic cholecystectomy
output_examples.output: A formatted letter containing provider header with contact details, patient demographics with member ID, CPT code 47562, ICD-10 code K80.20, clinical justification section detailing failed conservative management and recurrent biliary colic episodes, and a physician attestation block with signature line.

output_examples.input: Patient needing specialty TNF inhibitor after failing methotrexate and sulfasalazine
output_examples.output: A medication prior authorization letter listing the requested biologic drug, two failed formulary alternatives with specific reasons for discontinuation, supporting clinical evidence from ACR guidelines, rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis codes with severity indicators, and prescriber signature with NPI number.

output_examples.input: Patient with persistent neurological symptoms requiring brain MRI
output_examples.output: An imaging authorization letter with detailed neurological findings from recent examinations, explanation of why CT and X-ray are insufficient for the diagnostic question, proposed ICD-10 diagnosis codes for the differential, and urgency justification citing risk of delayed diagnosis.

best_practices: Provide complete clinical justification with specific findings, lab values, and treatment history rather than generic statements
Verify all ICD-10 and CPT codes against current coding standards before generating the final letter
Review carrier-specific requirements in the references directory and include all mandatory fields for the target insurance company
anti_patterns: Submitting letters with vague clinical justifications like 'medically necessary' without supporting evidence or failed alternative documentation
Using the generated letter without verifying that patient information, provider details, and procedure codes are accurate and current
Assuming the same letter template works for all insurance carriers without checking carrier-specific required fields and formatting rules

faq.question: What types of prior authorization requests does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports procedures, prescription medications, durable medical equipment (DME), and advanced imaging authorization requests.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide to generate a letter?
faq.answer: You need patient name and insurance ID, provider name and NPI, service description, ICD-10 diagnosis codes, clinical justification, and insurance carrier information.

faq.question: Can this skill submit letters directly to insurance companies?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates formatted letters as output files. You must submit them through your insurance carrier's provider portal, fax, or mail.

faq.question: Does this skill verify that a procedure or medication is medically necessary?
faq.answer: No. The skill formats the clinical justification you provide into a professional letter. Medical necessity determinations must be made by a qualified healthcare provider.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The Python script outputs plain text by default. DOCX and PDF output formats require installing the optional python-docx and jinja2 Python dependencies.

faq.question: How do I handle different insurance carrier requirements?
faq.answer: The references directory contains carrier-specific formatting rules and required fields for major insurers including Medicare, Medicaid, Blue Cross Blue Shield, UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, Humana, and Kaiser Permanente.
